title: Game Feel
creator: Steve Swink
description: A focused study of the moment-to-moment sensation of controlling a game. Swink separates input, response, context, polish, metaphor, and rules, then uses playable examples to show how changing them alters the experience.
useful: It gives designers a concrete vocabulary for something that is often discussed only as an instinct or a vague sense of polish.
audience: Designers and programmers tuning controls, feedback, movement, or action-game prototypes.
learning: Separate the components that produce game feel
Tune input and response through playable comparison
Connect audiovisual polish to underlying control
